title: A Prelude to Paperless Courts
primary_topics: Electronic Commerce Act and Rules on Electronic Evidence
sub_topics: Justice Robert Nicholson Federal Court of Australia lecture
Philippine Rules on Electronic Evidence (effective August 1, 2001)
electronic document as functional equivalent of paper
digital signature as functional equivalent of 'John Hancock'
Ten Commandments engraved in stone / Confucian wisdom in wooden tablets / paper as recent medium
DNA / genetic engineering / stem cells / cloning

stances.claim: The 21st century has shifted world preeminence 'from a battle of ideologies and weapons of mass destruction to one of trade dominance' — from government control to deregulation, state ownership to privatization, national sovereignty to globalization, protectionism to trade liberalization.
stances.rhetorical_move: structured-paired-shift framing (from X to Y) for global paradigm change

stances.claim: Biosciences breakthroughs (DNA testing, genetic engineering, stem cell cloning) promise that 'our children and grandchildren can look forward to much longer life spans of 150 or even 200 years' — even as they 'raise ethical and moral concerns.'

The law is 'said to be the most paper-dependent discipline in the world' — 'in no other field is the advice Put it in writing more prevalent than in the law'; statutes, contracts, parol evidence, statute of frauds, and best evidence rules all assume paper writing.
stances.rhetorical_move: structural-status quo framing (paper-dependency as legal-discipline default)

stances.claim: The ECA + Rules on Electronic Evidence 'eliminate the disparity of treatment between data messages and paper documents' — an electronic document is now 'the functional equivalent of a paper-based document; an electronic or digital signature, the functional equivalent of a traditionally penned John Hancock.'
stances.rhetorical_move: functional-equivalence doctrinal framing for electronic-evidence admissibility

stances.claim: The SC is honored to be 'one of the first tribunals in Asia to produce these Rules and, thereby, to keep in step with the electronic age' — though 'there is as yet no intention in the said law and Rules to eliminate paper as the primary medium of communication in the bar or the bench.'

laws_treaties: Republic Act No. 8792 (Electronic Commerce Act, June 11, 2000)
Philippine Rules on Electronic Evidence (effective August 1, 2001)
events: Justice Nicholson's Centenary Lecture on Paperless Courts (September 26, 2001)

signature_phrases: the most paper-dependent discipline in the world
the Ten Commandments may have been engraved in stone and Confucian wisdom in wooden tablets
the disparity of treatment between data messages and paper documents
to keep in step with the electronic age
the epochal prelude to paperless legal proceedings
decision_framework_signals: applies functional-equivalence doctrine (electronic = paper) as legal-modernization principle
uses structured-paired-shift framing (control → deregulation, ownership → privatization, sovereignty → globalization) as paradigm-recognition framework
frames incremental-reform (electronic evidence first, paperless courts as future possibility) as institutional-prudence approach
